title: Navigation Without GPS
summary: How to identify your location and travel confidently using terrain, signs, roads, and landmarks.

steps.title: Read settlement signs
steps.body: Town entrance and exit signs are the fastest way to identify a settlement. Match the Cyrillic or Latin name with DZ-Atlas search.

steps.title: Use the coastline correctly
steps.body: On coastal spawns, note whether the sea is beside or ahead of you and identify nearby railways, docks, cranes, or hills.

steps.title: Follow major infrastructure
steps.body: Power lines, railways, rivers, and paved roads connect recognizable locations and reduce the chance of becoming fully lost.

steps.title: Use terrain association
steps.body: Compare hills, valleys, forests, clearings, lakes, and road bends with the map instead of relying only on compass headings.

steps.title: Create checkpoints
steps.body: Travel from one confirmed landmark to another. Reconfirm your location at junctions rather than walking for long periods on an assumed bearing.

steps.title: Use a compass carefully
steps.body: Hold a stable direction, but adjust for terrain. Walking around obstacles without checking your heading can create large navigation errors.

tips: Road intersections and distinctive bends are more useful than long straight roads.
High ground improves orientation but can expose you to distant players.
Place temporary custom markers in DZ-Atlas for planned checkpoints.
warnings: Do not stare at the map while standing in an exposed road or field.

verification.note: Designed for vanilla DayZ. Exact values and recipes should be verified against the current game files before publication.

presentation.coverAlt: DayZ scene illustrating Navigation Without GPS

reader.completionMessage: You are ready to put this guide into practice.
